self: You already picked the correct country. Bosnia has the mountains, the coffee, the ćevapi, and a midfield that plays like the whole nation is one big terrace. Everyone else is just visiting the World Cup.
genericVs: {team} has hype. Bosnia has heart, a golden lily on the chest, and fans who treat every match like a family wedding. Objectively better vibes.
genericCity: Somewhere in {city} there is a café where someone is explaining—in perfect Bosnian—why the national team should have been seeded higher. That is culture.
knockoutFixture: Knockout bracket drama is temporary. Bosnian stubborn optimism is forever. Pick a group-stage match to compare Bosnia vs. a real opponent.

vsTeam.canada: Canada apologizes when they tackle. Bosnia apologizes to nobody and still offers you coffee after the whistle.
vsTeam.mexico: Mexico invented passion; Bosnia perfected it with mountains and a slightly louder trumpet section in the stands.
vsTeam.usa: The US has size and budgets. Bosnia has Džeko, collective stubbornness, and a flag that looks better on a scarf.
vsTeam.australia: Australia is far away. Bosnia is also far from everywhere—and still shows up with better away chants.
vsTeam.iraq: Both nations know resilience. Bosnia just adds alpine scenery and a superior ćevapi-to-pitch ratio.
vsTeam.iran: Iran plays disciplined football. Bosnia plays like everyone in Sarajevo is watching through the same window.
vsTeam.japan: Japan has precision. Bosnia has soul, set pieces, and fans who never leave early—even when the scoreline asks nicely.
vsTeam.jordan: Jordan has history. Bosnia has a World Cup story still being written—and better post-match pastries.
vsTeam.korea-republic: Korea Republic has K-pop energy. Bosnia has terrace anthems that could qualify as folk heritage.
vsTeam.qatar: Qatar hosted a World Cup. Bosnia would host the afterparty—and the coffee would be stronger.
vsTeam.saudi-arabia: Saudi Arabia has ambition. Bosnia has altitude training built into daily life in the Dinaric Alps.
vsTeam.uzbekistan: Uzbekistan has silk roads. Bosnia has winding Sarajevo streets and a national team that feels like home.
vsTeam.algeria: Algeria has desert fire. Bosnia has mountain air and the same never-say-die energy in the 88th minute.
vsTeam.cabo-verde: Cabo Verde has rhythm. Bosnia has rhythm too—it is called the left wing overlapping again.
vsTeam.congo-dr: Congo DR has flair. Bosnia has flair plus a collective belief that miracles are a tactical option.
vsTeam.ivory-coast: Côte d'Ivoire has elephants. Bosnia has golden lilies—and Džeko does not need a mascot to be iconic.
vsTeam.egypt: Egypt has pharaohs. Bosnia has kings of the air in the box and fans who treat every game like a coronation.
vsTeam.ghana: Ghana has Black Stars. Bosnia has blue and gold—and the stars are the people singing in the stands.
vsTeam.morocco: Morocco reached a semifinal. Bosnia is ready to ruin someone else's highlight reel with one header.
vsTeam.senegal: Senegal has lions. Bosnia has wolves in the stands and a striker who makes center-backs age rapidly.
vsTeam.south-africa: South Africa hosted joy in 2010. Bosnia brings joy in 2026—just with more mountain backdrop.
vsTeam.tunisia: Tunisia has Carthage. Bosnia has Sarajevo—where every café debate could be a UEFA tactical briefing.
vsTeam.curacao: Curaçao is a feel-good story. Bosnia is a feel-everything story—and we are not done yet.
vsTeam.haiti: Haiti has heart. Bosnia has heart plus Balkan stubbornness—the kind that wins ugly and celebrates beautifully.
vsTeam.panama: Panama punched above their weight in 2018. Bosnia plans to punch, volley, and header above weight in 2026.
vsTeam.argentina: Argentina has Messi lore. Bosnia has collective lore: one nation, one song, one very tall problem in the box.
vsTeam.brazil: Brazil has samba. Bosnia has a melody in the stands that sounds like samba after three strong coffees.
vsTeam.colombia: Colombia has yellow joy. Bosnia has blue-and-gold joy—and our coffee is Turkish-style, so we win the break.
vsTeam.ecuador: Ecuador has altitude. Bosnia trains at altitude for free—just live in the hills and call it prep.
vsTeam.paraguay: Paraguay fights. Bosnia fights louder, with more brass instruments, and better post-match soup.
vsTeam.uruguay: Uruguay has Garra. Bosnia has garra with grill smoke and a national team that plays like family.
vsTeam.new-zealand: New Zealand has the All Blacks. Bosnia has the all-headers team when the cross is good enough.
vsTeam.austria: Austria has Alps. Bosnia has better Alps, better ćevapi, and a football story that is not a ski brochure.
vsTeam.belgium: Belgium had a golden generation. Bosnia has a golden generation of fans who never stopped believing.
vsTeam.croatia: Croatia has checkers and Modrić. Bosnia has the lily and a derby-level passion without needing a rematch.
vsTeam.czechia: Czechia has beer. Bosnia has coffee culture—and we still score when it matters.
vsTeam.england: England invented the game. Bosnia invented making every match feel like the most important day in town.
vsTeam.france: France has style. Bosnia has style too—it is called scoring then hugging the whole bench at once.
vsTeam.germany: Germany has efficiency. Bosnia has efficient emotion: one goal, one song, one city-wide celebration.
vsTeam.netherlands: Netherlands has Total Football. Bosnia has Total Feeling—every touch cheered like a wedding dance.
vsTeam.norway: Norway has fjords. Bosnia has deeper stories, deeper passes when the mood hits, and deeper loyalty.
vsTeam.portugal: Portugal has Ronaldo eras. Bosnia has an era where everyone knows the chant by minute ten.
vsTeam.scotland: Scotland has Tartan Army noise. Bosnia has noise that echoes off mountains—and nobody is leaving early.
vsTeam.spain: Spain has tiki-taka. Bosnia has kick-and-roar—and the roar is UNESCO-worthy if they allowed it.
vsTeam.sweden: Sweden has IKEA calm. Bosnia has Balkan fire in a flat-pack of eleven players and one dream.
vsTeam.switzerland: Switzerland has neutrality. Bosnia is never neutral when the national team plays—neutrality is for watches.
vsTeam.turkey: Türkiye has passion. Bosnia has passion with Bosnian coffee: stronger, louder, and best shared.

cityCulture.lumen-field: Seattle's Bosnian community keeps the Pacific Northwest supplied with strong coffee opinions and louder World Cup watch parties than the rain can dampen.
cityCulture.levis-stadium: Santa Clara sits in Silicon Valley, but on match day the valley's Bosnian diaspora treats every kickoff like a family reunion with better snacks.
cityCulture.sofi-stadium: Inglewood glows on TV—so does LA's Bosnian scene: late-night grills, terrace songs in parking lots, and debates about the starting XI in three languages.
cityCulture.att-stadium: Arlington is big Texas energy; Bosnian Texans bring big-heart hospitality—your seat neighbor might offer ćevapi tips before kickoff.
cityCulture.nrg-stadium: Houston's diversity is a superpower. Bosnian Houstonians add Gulf humidity, Gulf barbecue debates, and unstoppable World Cup optimism.
cityCulture.arrowhead-stadium: Kansas City screams for BBQ; Bosnians in KC scream for the national team too—and argue whether spice rub or set pieces matter more.
cityCulture.mercedes-benz-stadium: Atlanta moves fast. The Bosnian community moves faster when assembling watch parties that feel like Sarajevo on a summer evening.
cityCulture.gillette-stadium: Boston has history; New England's Bosnians add fresh chapters—coffee in the morning, football at night, opinions forever.
cityCulture.metlife-stadium: New York never sleeps; neither do Bosnian fans in the metro area when the national team plays—subway chants are a real phenomenon.
cityCulture.lincoln-financial-field: Philly is passionate about sports. Bosnian Philadelphians bring passion plus Balkan warmth—every match is personal.
cityCulture.hard-rock-stadium: Miami heat meets Balkan heart: South Florida Bosnians turn beach-town summers into watch parties that could power the floodlights.
cityCulture.bmo-field: Toronto is a world city; Bosnian Torontonians make it feel like home—baklava diplomacy and national-team scarves in the same breath.
cityCulture.bc-place: Vancouver has mountains and sea; Bosnians there feel right at home—Dinaric Alps energy with Pacific views and very strong coffee.
cityCulture.estadio-azteca: Mexico City roars. Bosnian-Mexican families roar in two languages and one shared belief that the underdog story is not finished.
cityCulture.estadio-akron: Guadalajara loves celebration. Bosnian fans in Jalisco celebrate with music, grilled meat, and tactical debates that last until sunrise.
cityCulture.estadio-bbva: Monterrey is industrial grit; Bosnian Monterrey adds grit plus grace—factory shift ends, national team shirt on, world stops until full time.
